text: Fill in the blanks on xenobiotic metabolism, oxidative stress, and antioxidant defense.
questions: Phase I biotransformation reactions are primarily catalysed by the *cytochrome P450/CYP450/CYP* monooxygenase system located in the *smooth endoplasmic reticulum/smooth ER/SER* of hepatocytes.
At therapeutic doses, paracetamol is safely conjugated, but at toxic doses the reactive metabolite *NAPQI/N-acetyl-p-benzoquinone imine* accumulates and depletes hepatic *glutathione/GSH*.
The antidote for paracetamol toxicity is *N-acetylcysteine/NAC* because it replenishes *glutathione/GSH* stores in the liver.
The enzyme *superoxide dismutase/SOD* converts superoxide radicals to *hydrogen peroxide/H2O2*, which is then detoxified by *catalase/Catalase* or *glutathione peroxidase/GPx*.
Phase II reactions involve *conjugation/Conjugation* of the xenobiotic with polar molecules such as glucuronic acid, sulphate, or *glutathione/GSH* to increase *water solubility/hydrophilicity*.
